stem:
f(x) = 3x2 + 48x + 200

The function g is defined by g(x) = f(x + 4). For what value of x does g(x) reach its minimum?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The minimum of f occurs at its vertex, x = −b/(2a) = −48/6 = −8. Since g(x) = f(x + 4), the graph of g is the graph of f shifted 4 units to the left. So g reaches its minimum at x = −8 − 4 = −12.

Choice A is the minimum location of f itself, not of g. Choices B and D arise from shifting the wrong direction or doubling the shift.
